course_title: Module 1: OCP & ORv3: History and Impact
course_subtitle: Welcome to module 1: History and Impact, where you will explore the transformative journey of the Open Compute Project and its open-source design philosophy.
course_description_html: Welcome to module 1: History and Impact, where you will explore the transformative journey of the Open Compute Project and its open-source design philosophy. In this module, you will gain insights into the evolution of Open Rack from its earlier versions to the current ORv3 standard, understand key milestones in its development, and discuss its significant impact on data center design and innovation. You will also learn how Open Rack v3 addresses the demands of modern AI workloads while promoting sustainability and efficiency in data center operations.

blocks.body_html:
Special thanks to the Open Compute Project community and contributors for providing the history, data, and real-world examples used throughout this module. Their work drives the evolution of open-source hardware standards worldwide.

Andrew Lui, Acbel

Gary Sapia, Analog Devices

Howie Granat, AEI

Nrupathunga SR, Molex

Robin Kleinegrauthof, Mennekes

Roy Davis, OmniOn Power

Tim Pak, Foxconn Interconnect Technology

Vijay Swarna, JTEC Industries

Zach Schimke, Penguin Solutions.

blocks.heading_html: By the end of this module, you will be able to:

items_html: Describe the mission of the Open Compute Project and its open-source design philosophy
Explain how Open Rack has evolved from ORv1 and ORv2 to the current ORv3 standard
Summarize key development milestones and industry adoption of ORv3 from 2016 to 2025
Discuss the impact of ORv3 on data center design, standarization, and innovation
Identify how ORv3 enables greater density and meets the demands of AI workloads

blocks.body_html: The Open Compute Project was created to tackle traditional data center challenges through open-source collaboration. In this module, you’ll learn how the Open Rack standard has evolved from ORv1 to ORv3, what milestones shaped its development, and how ORv3 is helping data centers scale for high-performance computing, AI, and sustainability.

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

lessons.title: The Evolution of Open Rack: From ORv1 to ORv3

blocks.body_html: The Open Compute Project was founded in 2011 to tackle inefficiencies in traditional data center designs. The Open Rack standard has evolved through multiple versions to keep pace with growing power and cooling demands in hyperscale and enterprise environments.

items.title: ORv1 (2011)
items.description_html:
Introduced 12V DC centralized power shelf and busbars to reduce cables and improve serviceability.

https://github.com/facebookarchive/opencompute/blob/master/open_rack/spec/Open_Compute_Project_Open_Rack_v1.0.pdf

items.title: ORv2 (2014-2015)
items.description_html:
Improved power/rack design for hyperscalers using 21-inch servers and DC power, but had limited broader adoption.  

https://www.opencompute.org/documents/facebook-v2-open-rack-v2-092-spec-and-cad-release

items.title: ORv3 (2016–2022)
items.description_html:
Developed to address limitations of ORv2 with higher power density (up to 30 kW/rack originally), better cooling, and flexibility for both hyperscale and enterprise needs.  

https://www.opencompute.org/documents/open-rack-base-specification-version-3-rev1-1-030524-pdf

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

blocks.body_html: As demands for high-performance computing and AI workloads grew, the OCP community worked together to make ORv3 more efficient, flexible, and ready for widespread adoption. This timeline highlights key moments from early planning to mass deployment.
Click on the timeline to learn more.

items.description_html: Community, including Meta, Google, and Microsoft, identified needs for higher power density and improved cooling.
Advanced Energy and others developed ORv3-compliant 48V DC power shelves for efficiency and high power demands.
OCP Global Summit showcased 97.5% efficient power shelves with up to 36 kW in 2OU form factor. ORv3 finalized and ready for mass production.
Major adoption by hyperscalers and enterprises. Examples: Meta’s Catalina AI Compute Shelf and NVIDIA’s GB200 platforms pushing ORv3 to support 140kW per rack and beyond.

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

lessons.title: ORv3’s Impact: Efficiency, Scalability and Flexibility

blocks.body_html: ORv3 is more than just a hardware standard. By addressing real-world challenges like power efficiency, cooling, and standardization, it has helped transform how hyperscale and enterprise data centers operate
Click on each area of impact to learn more.

items.title: Power Efficiency
items.description_html: Up to 30 kW per rack and up to 140 kW in advanced setups. 48V DC shelves reduce losses and points of failure.

items.title: Cooling Capabilities
items.description_html: Liquid cooling manifolds and wider racks improve airflow and support HPC and AI.

items.title: Flexibility & Adoption
items.description_html: Supports 21-inch and 19-inch equipment for hybrid deployments. Preconfigured solutions reduce custom engineering.

items.title: Standardization
items.description_html: Vendor-neutral ecosystem with 400+ companies contributing and adopting the standard.

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

blocks.body_html: Modern data centers need to run powerful AI and high-performance workloads while managing heat and sustainability goals. ORv3 was designed to deliver higher power density, integrate advanced liquid cooling, and reduce environmental impact.
Click on each support feature to learn more:

items.title: Liquid Cooling & Cold Plates
items.description_html: Standards for manifolds and cold plates address limitations of air cooling as rack power exceeds 50 kW.

items.title: 21-inch Rack Design
items.description_html: Wider racks improve airflow and thermal management compared to 19-inch EIA racks.

items.title: Sustainability
items.description_html: Optimizes energy use, supports circular design, and reduces water usage. Added as an official focus area in 2023.

items.title: AI Workloads
items.description_html: Supports dense GPU systems, like Meta's Catalina and Clemente shelves.

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

blocks.body_html: Test your knowledge from this module. Select the best answer for each question, then submit.

blocks.question_html: Who founded the Open Compute Project in 2011?
blocks.feedback_correct_html: Correct! Facebook (now Meta), Intel, and Rackspace launched OCP to share open-source hardware for more efficient data centers.
blocks.feedback_incorrect_html: Not quite. Facebook (now Meta), Intel, and Rackspace launched OCP to share open-source hardware for more efficient data centers.

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

blocks.question_html: What did ORv1 introduce to improve power distribution?
blocks.feedback_correct_html: Correct! ORv1 centralized power with a 12V DC power shelf and busbars to reduce cables and improve serviceability.
blocks.feedback_incorrect_html: Not quite. ORv1 centralized power with a 12V DC power shelf and busbars to reduce cables and improve serviceability.

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

blocks.question_html: Which trend drove the need for ORv3?
blocks.feedback_correct_html: Correct! HPC and AI workloads required higher power density and better cooling, leading to ORv3 development.
blocks.feedback_incorrect_html: Not quite. HPC and AI workloads required higher power density and better cooling, leading to ORv3 development.

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

blocks.question_html: How does ORv3 handle advanced cooling needs?

choices.text_html: Integrates liquid cooling manifolds and cold plates
Uses wider racks to generate more heat

blocks.feedback_correct_html: Correct! ORv3 integrates liquid cooling solutions and wider racks to handle high-density workloads efficiently
blocks.feedback_incorrect_html: Not quite. ORv3 integrates liquid cooling solutions and wider racks to handle high-density workloads efficiently

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

blocks.body_html: In this module, you learned how to:

items_html: Describe the mission of the Open Compute Project and its open-source approach
Explain the evolution from ORv1 and ORv2 to the ORv3 standard
Summarize major development milestones and industry adoption of ORv3
Discuss the impact of ORv3 on modern data center design and innovation
Identify how ORv3 supports advanced cooling, sustainability, and AI workloads
